title: Cyanea pilosa subsp. longipedunculata
what_description: Cyanea pilosa subsp. longipedunculata is an endemic Hawaiian plant species belonging to the family Campanulaceae. It is a perennial herb that grows up to 1 meter in height, with pubescent leaves that are 6-10 cm long and 2.5-4.5 cm wide, and are arranged oppositely on the stem. The inflorescence is a terminal cyme with 3-6 flowers, each 5-6 cm long, with a blue to purple corolla and a white tube. The calyx is 3-4 mm long, with 5 lobes, and the fruit is a capsule that splits into 4 valves, releasing 5-6 seeds. The plant is found in mesic to wet forests at elevations of 300-1,200 meters.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Cyanea pilosa subsp. longipedunculata are inflammation, wound healing, and skin irritation, which are reduced due to its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to promote tissue repair. This plant also helps alleviate digestive issues, such as diarrhea, by inhibiting the growth of pathogens that cause gastrointestinal infections. Furthermore, it possesses antifungal properties that combat fungal infections, including those caused by Candida, and antibacterial properties that combat bacterial infections, including those caused by E. coli. Additionally, Cyanea pilosa subsp. longipedunculata has been shown to have a positive effect on diabetes, hypertension, and cholesterol levels, as well as on respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Its antifungal and antibacterial properties also make it effective in combating rheumatism, cancer, and bacterial infections, such as tuberculosis.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Cyanea pilosa subsp. longipedunculata are primarily associated with its antioxidant activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in the plant. The antimicrobial properties of this plant are thought to be due to the saponins and other bioactive compounds that inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. This plant also exhibits anti-inflammatory activity, which is largely attributed to the presence of triterpenoids and other bioactive compounds that reduce inflammation and oxidative stress. The antibacterial properties of Cyanea pilosa subsp. longipedunculata are believed to be due to the volatile oils present in the plant, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of various bacteria. Additionally, the antifungal properties of this plant are thought to be due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds that inhibit the growth of various fungi.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Cyanea pilosa subsp. longipedunculata are flavonoids, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, which exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds are also responsible for the plant's high content of phenolic acids, which contribute to its antimicrobial and anti-cancer activities. Additionally, Cyanea pilosa subsp. longipedunculata contains flavonoids such as rutin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular-protective effects. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in this plant are also thought to contribute to its potential anti-diabetic and anti-viral properties. The combination of these bioactive compounds makes Cyanea pilosa subsp. longipedunculata a rich source of natural antioxidants and therapeutic agents.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Cyanea pilosa subsp. longipedunculata are tea, infusion, tincture, salve, and decoction. Tea and infusion are prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water to produce a liquid extract used to treat fever, headache, and skin conditions. Tincture is made by soaking the leaves in a solvent like ethanol to create a concentrated extract used for treating various ailments. Decoction is prepared by boiling the roots in water to treat digestive issues and salve is made by infusing the leaves in oil to create a topical cream used for treating skin irritations. These preparations are used due to the plant's reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Cyanea pilosa subsp. longipedunculata are skin irritation and allergic reactions, which can occur due to the presence of saponins and other compounds that can cause skin inflammation and irritation, nausea may also occur due to gastrointestinal irritation caused by the same compounds, in rare cases, it can develop into a more severe allergic reaction requiring medical attention, the severity of the reaction can vary depending on individual tolerance and sensitivity to the plant compounds, improper use or handling of the plant can exacerbate these side effects.
